preamble: The strengthening of the labio-velar glide [w] into a voiced velar stop [ɡ] is a well-documented fortition process. In Western Romance, Germanic *w was typically adapted as [ɡw] and subsequently simplified to [ɡ] in French, while other languages retained the labial component or shifted to [v].

examples.note: Germanic *w adopted as [gw] then simplified to [g].

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
Alkire, T., & Rosen, C. (2010). Romance Philology.
Watson, J. C. (2002). The Phonology and Morphology of Arabic.
